profile.name: Calvin Hou
profile.title: Electrical Engineering major
profile.scopeBrief: EE @ Georgia Tech · robotics · PCBs · embedded control. Pick a channel below.

about.paragraph: I'm an Electrical Engineering student with a strong background in building reliable robotics + hardware systems. My passion mostly lies in building self-sufficient autonomous systems. My technical background consists mostly of various hardware projects, designing, building, debugging, and validating.

My work background includes the validation and assembly of cutting-edge AI server hardware, robot design and development, and electrical system design for various on-campus competition teams, such as our FSAE and Rocketry Teams.

projects.title: Custom Media Controller & Keyboard
projects.description:
Designed and fabricated a custom PCB using KiCad and STM32. 
Utilizes CherryMX-style switches and a rotary encoder for volume control.

Handwired a keyboard using a simple diode matrix to prevent ghosting, with a Teensy 2.0 microcontroller for reliable USB keyscanning.

projects.title: Pulse FRC Pit Display
projects.description: Designed and optimized UX/UI for a multi-functional pit display supporting FRC teams during competition. Reached 4,000+ users in the first week.

experience.title: Research and Design Intern
experience.description:
Engineered and validated a next-generation (V2) liquid cooling system by leading testing and integration efforts on the Liquid Pump Unit (LPU), and Coolant Distribution Unit (CDU). Conducted rigorous pressure and leak testing to ensure system integrity, achieving a 100% pass rate across validation cycles. Assembled and wired multiple CDU units, integrating distributed leak detection sensors and verifying functionality through controlled fault injection and response testing.

Designed and implemented temperature monitoring solutions using welded thermistors, collecting and analyzing large datasets to benchmark V2 performance against prior V1 systems. Identified thermal inefficiencies and introduced targeted airflow optimizations—including custom air guides, which improved radiator cooling uniformity and reduced temperature variance across critical components.

experience.title: Advanced Robotics Centre Research Intern
experience.description:
Worked under researcher Marcelo Ang to explore emerging applications in humanoid and soft robotics, contributing to early-stage research on automating cargo lashing operations in high-throughput maritime ports such as Singapore. The project aimed to replace a traditionally manual, labor-intensive process with a scalable robotic solution.

Developed a functional proof-of-concept system using a custom-built 3DOF robotic arm to simulate automated lashing and unlashing tasks. Self-taught C++ to implement low-level servo control, and engineered a custom hardware controller to interface with the system. Designed and deployed inverse kinematics and velocity control algorithms, improving end-effector positioning accuracy and motion smoothness by an estimated 40% compared to initial direct-control methods.

Demonstrated the feasibility of automating repetitive port operations using cost-effective, widely accessible components, validating that robust performance could be achieved without specialized hardware. This work established a foundation for scaling toward higher-DOF systems and more complex real-world deployment scenarios, supporting broader efforts in industrial robotics automation.
